title: A deliberation: the council mends its own rules
topik: The council wants to improve how it deliberates - better, but not bloated. What should change?

beats.text: The council convenes. Five advisors take their seats - the **Simplicity** lens, the **Maintainability** lens, the **Usability** lens, the **Scalability** lens, and the blunt **Contrarian** - each pressing one angle.
**Don't add new rules.** Fewer rules, easier to use. A lot of what exists could simply be deleted.
The real defect: the rules are **written many times, in many places**, until they contradict each other. Write them once, in one home. Fix that first.
For whoever reads the result: **put the verdict at the very top.** Don't make people read a wall of text before they find the answer.
Don't always debate at length. When everyone already agrees, **conclude immediately**. Debate only when there is a genuine split - save the time.

Careful: the five of us share the same "brain." We can look like we agree **not because we are right, but because we reason alike.** And a "78 out of 100" confidence number is misleading - it looks precise but it is just a guess.

The Chair weighs the opinions. Nearly all point one way: **"simplify, don't add."** But two things still split us: cut down to how many voices? drop the confidence number or not? So the debate continues on those two.
I **withdraw** my earlier idea (cut to just two voices) - that is only another tool we already have. The Scalability lens's way is better. But on the confidence number, I **hold**: drop it.
I **revise**: not merge into one file, but give each thing one clear "home." What I **hold**: tidy up before adding anything.
I **turn around**: agreed, the confidence number should go - the Contrarian is right, it is a guess dressed up as a number. What I **hold**: reject every new addition.
I **hold**: debate only when there is a split, and the contrarian **must always speak** even when everyone agrees - insurance against being wrong together.
I drop the "ask permission mid-run" idea. The 0-100 number I replace with three simple bands: **firm / lean / shaky**. What I **hold**: verdict at the top.
I decide - not by counting the most votes. The irony: the best way to improve this council is to **simplify and sharpen**, not add. Seven changes:
list: Each rule lives in one place - never repeated until it conflicts.
Define "skip" clearly: when to hold no meeting at all, when to skip only the debate.
Drop the 0-100 number; use three bands: firm / lean / shaky - plus "what would change this view."
Debate only when there is a split; if everyone agrees, conclude.
The contrarian always speaks; if it dissents, show its voice in full.
Put the verdict at the top; keep the functional lens names as the brand.
Hold back - don't add what isn't needed.

beats.text: **Certainty: firm.** Remember: "four of five agree" is not a vote won - they just happen to think the same way. The council is closed.
